concepts.name: Fractions and Decimals
concepts.description: Understanding, converting, and performing arithmetic operations on fractional and decimal values.

concepts.name: Ratios and Proportions
concepts.description: Comparing quantities and solving for unknowns by setting equivalent ratios.

concepts.name: Order of Operations
concepts.description: Evaluating mathematical expressions systematically using standard priority rules.

concepts.name: Basic One-Variable Equations
concepts.description: Solving simple algebraic equations for a single unknown variable using inverse operations.

concepts.name: Linear Equations
concepts.description: Graphing and solving equations of the first degree, understanding slope and y-intercept.

concepts.name: Linear Inequalities
concepts.description: Solving and graphing inequalities to find ranges of valid solutions.

concepts.name: Systems of Linear Equations
concepts.description: Finding the common solution to two or more linear equations simultaneously.

concepts.name: Basics of Functions
concepts.description: Understanding function notation, domain, range, and the concept of inputs mapping to unique outputs.

concepts.name: Exponent Rules
concepts.description: Simplifying expressions using the properties of integer and rational exponents.

concepts.name: Polynomial Operations
concepts.description: Adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing algebraic expressions with multiple terms.

concepts.name: Radical Expressions
concepts.description: Simplifying and operating on expressions containing square roots and higher-order roots.

concepts.name: Factoring Polynomials
concepts.description: Decomposing polynomials into products of simpler polynomials.

concepts.name: Quadratic Equations
concepts.description: Solving second-degree polynomial equations using factoring, the quadratic formula, and completing the square.

concepts.name: Descriptive Statistics
concepts.description: Summarizing data using measures of center (mean, median, mode) and spread (variance, standard deviation).

concepts.name: Basic Probability
concepts.description: Calculating the likelihood of simple and compound events using combinatorial logic.

concepts.name: Lines and Angles
concepts.description: Understanding geometric properties of intersecting lines, parallel lines, and angle relationships.

concepts.name: Triangles and Congruence
concepts.description: Proving triangles congruent or similar and applying properties of interior and exterior angles.

concepts.name: Right Triangle Geometry
concepts.description: Using the Pythagorean theorem and special right triangle rules to find missing side lengths.

concepts.name: Geometry of Circles
concepts.description: Analyzing chords, secants, tangents, and calculating arc lengths and sector areas.

concepts.name: Area and Volume
concepts.description: Calculating the two-dimensional area and three-dimensional volume of geometric figures.

concepts.name: Geometric Transformations
concepts.description: Translating, rotating, reflecting, and dilating figures on a coordinate plane.

concepts.name: Rational Expressions
concepts.description: Simplifying, adding, and solving equations involving algebraic fractions.

concepts.name: Complex Numbers
concepts.description: Understanding imaginary numbers and performing arithmetic operations on complex values.

concepts.name: Exponential Functions
concepts.description: Modeling growth and decay mathematically using equations where variables appear in the exponent.

concepts.name: Logarithmic Functions
concepts.description: Evaluating logs, using log properties, and solving logarithmic equations as the inverse of exponentials.

concepts.name: Sequences and Series
concepts.description: Identifying arithmetic and geometric patterns, finding nth terms, and summing finite and infinite series.

concepts.name: Trigonometry Basics
concepts.description: Using sine, cosine, and tangent functions on the unit circle and in right triangles.

concepts.name: Trigonometric Identities
concepts.description: Proving mathematical equivalence using fundamental trigonometric relationships like the Pythagorean identity.

concepts.name: Probability Distributions
concepts.description: Modeling random variables and using normal and binomial distributions to calculate probabilities.

concepts.name: Vectors
concepts.description: Representing magnitude and direction mathematically, and computing dot products and vector addition.

concepts.name: Matrices
concepts.description: Organizing data into rectangular arrays and performing matrix multiplication and inversion.

concepts.name: Conic Sections
concepts.description: Graphing and analyzing parabolas, ellipses, and hyperbolas derived from slicing a cone.

concepts.name: Limits and Continuity
concepts.description: Evaluating the behavior of functions as inputs approach specific values or infinity.

concepts.name: Derivatives
concepts.description: Calculating instantaneous rates of change and the slope of the tangent line using formal rules.

concepts.name: Applications of Derivatives
concepts.description: Using derivatives to solve optimization and related rates problems, and to sketch curves.

concepts.name: Integrals
concepts.description: Finding antiderivatives and computing definite integrals to determine accumulated quantities.

concepts.name: Applications of Integrals
concepts.description: Applying integration to find areas between curves, volumes of solids of revolution, and net change.

concepts.name: Inferential Statistics
concepts.description: Drawing conclusions about populations from sample data using confidence intervals and hypothesis testing.
